title: Google Analytics Fundamentals
description: Learn how to track, analyze, and report on website traffic using Google Analytics 4 (GA4). Understand user behavior, track conversions, and build custom reports to drive data-informed marketing decisions.

seo_metadata.title: Google Analytics Fundamentals Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Master Google Analytics 4 (GA4). Learn to track website traffic, user behavior, conversions, and build custom reports.

learning_outcomes: Navigate the Google Analytics 4 interface and understand the event-based data model
Install GA4 on a website using Google Tag Manager
Analyze user acquisition and engagement reports
Set up custom events and mark them as conversions
Create Explorations to perform advanced data analysis

modules.title: Introduction to GA4
modules.description: Understanding the new era of analytics.

lessons.title: The Event-Based Model
lessons.content:
Everything is an Event

Unlike Universal Analytics which relied on pageviews and sessions, GA4 tracks everything as an Event (a pageview, a scroll, a click, a video view). This provides a more flexible way to measure user interactions.

lessons.title: Installation Basics
lessons.content:
Google Tag Manager

The best way to install GA4 is via Google Tag Manager (GTM). GTM acts as a container on your website, allowing you to deploy the GA4 configuration tag without needing a developer to alter the site's code.

lessons.title: Acquisition Reports
lessons.content:
Where Are They Coming From?

The Acquisition reports (User and Traffic) show you how people find your website—whether via organic search, paid ads, social media, or direct traffic.

lessons.title: Engagement Reports
lessons.content:
What Are They Doing?

Engagement reports show you which pages are most popular, how long users stay, and what events they trigger. The 'Engagement Rate' replaces the old 'Bounce Rate' metric.

lessons.title: Tracking Conversions
lessons.content:
Measuring Success

To track success, you define specific events (like 'purchase' or 'generate_lead') and mark them as Conversions in the admin panel. This allows you to see which traffic sources drive the most value.

lessons.title: Explorations
lessons.content:
Custom Reporting

The 'Explore' tab in GA4 allows you to build custom funnels, path explorations, and free-form data tables to answer complex questions that the standard reports cannot.

mcqs.question: What is the primary data model used in Google Analytics 4 (GA4)?
What is the recommended tool for installing GA4 on a website?
Which GA4 report tells you how users initially found your website (e.g., Organic Search, Paid Video)?
In GA4, what metric has largely replaced the traditional 'Bounce Rate' to provide a more positive view of user interaction?
How do you track a Conversion in GA4?

options.content: By creating a specific Destination URL goal
By identifying an important Event (like 'purchase') and toggling it 'Mark as conversion'
It happens automatically for every click
By paying Google for Conversion Tracking

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of the 'Explore' section in GA4?

options.content: To learn how to use Google Ads
To create advanced, custom reports like funnel analysis and path explorations

mcqs.question: In GA4, a pageview is considered:
If you want to see which specific pages on your website are viewed the most, which report should you look at?
What is a 'parameter' in the context of a GA4 Event?

options.content: Additional data sent along with an event to provide context (e.g., sending 'link_url' with a 'click' event)
The total budget of the account

mcqs.question: Why is it important to link GA4 with Google Ads?

options.content: To get a discount on ads
To import GA4 conversions into Google Ads for bidding optimization and to see ad cost data in GA4
To increase your website's organic SEO ranking
